skill.name: cookmode-v2-source-of-truth
skill.description: Documents and explains the CookMode V2 codebase as it exists, including architecture, file locations, component behavior, and data flow.
skill.summary: Creates factual technical maps of the CookMode V2 implementation.
skill.author: AdamFehse

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the skill files and were dismissed as false positives. The weak cryptography alerts are substring matches inside normal prose, and the backtick execution alerts are markdown fences, inline file paths, or sample output text. No malicious intent, prompt injection attempt, network access, executable code, or real command execution was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm names, but the reviewed lines contain ordinary documentation words and descriptions, not cryptographic code or configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose such as descriptions, documentation headings, and file names. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing, encryption, or authentication logic appears in these locations.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Static Matches Are Markdown Only
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged backtick usage, but the reviewed content uses markdown code fences, inline filenames, and sample documentation. There is no Ruby code and no shell command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every referenced backtick occurrence is inside markdown formatting or inline technical names. The file is a prompt-only markdown skill and contains no Ruby interpreter context or executable shell wrapper.

content.value_statement: Teams need accurate facts before changing an existing application. This skill maps CookMode V2 files, components, architecture, and data flow without adding recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Explains CookMode V2 components and where they are implemented.
Maps frontend files, hooks, utilities, constants, styles, and database schema.
Describes React component flow from the app root to recipe views.
Explains Supabase-backed recipe state and real-time synchronization.
Produces factual markdown documentation with file paths and line numbers.
Avoids design critiques unless the user explicitly asks for them.
limitations: Only covers CookMode V2 information described by the skill.
Does not perform debugging or root cause analysis by default.
Does not propose refactors, optimizations, or future features by default.
Requires access to the target repository for current line numbers.

use_cases.title: Learn the application structure
use_cases.description: Understand key CookMode V2 directories, files, and component responsibilities before editing code.

use_cases.title: Locate implementation details
use_cases.description: Find where a feature is implemented and how related hooks, utilities, and tables interact.

use_cases.title: Create factual reference docs
use_cases.description: Generate concise architecture notes and feature maps from the current CookMode V2 implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Find a component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single implementation detail
prompt_templates.prompt: Where is the RecipeModal component implemented, and what does it do?

prompt_templates.title: Explain a feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Document how recipe scaling works in CookMode V2, including file paths and line numbers.

prompt_templates.title: Trace data flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request across hooks and persistence
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how recipe status changes move from the UI through hooks to Supabase and real-time updates.

prompt_templates.title: Map architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a full current-state reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a current-state architecture map for CookMode V2 covering directories, components, hooks, data flow, and database tables.

output_examples.output: A concise feature note that states what RecipeGrid displays, where it is located, and which components or hooks it connects to.

output_examples.input: How does recipe data flow through the app?
output: A factual data-flow summary from Supabase initialization to recipe state management.
A list of involved hooks and components.
Notes on optimistic UI updates and real-time synchronization.

output_examples.output: A current-state summary of the Pico CSS approach, semantic HTML usage, remaining custom CSS, and related file locations.

best_practices: Ask about one feature or component when you need precise file references.
Request line numbers when you plan to inspect or edit the implementation.
State when you want recommendations, because the skill defaults to factual documentation.
anti_patterns: Using this skill as a general React or Supabase tutor.
Expecting automatic refactoring advice without explicitly requesting it.
Relying on it for current line numbers without repository access.

faq.question: What does this skill document?
faq.answer: It documents the current CookMode V2 codebase, including files, components, hooks, data flow, and database tables.

faq.question: Does it change code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for documentation and explanation, not implementation or edits.

faq.question: Can it suggest improvements?
faq.answer: Only when the user explicitly asks. Its default behavior is to describe what exists.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No executable command behavior was found in the skill files. The audit found markdown examples, not command execution.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static alerts were false positives, but the report keeps a low rating to document the reviewed findings.
